• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

hw-4 code

First i will load library and clean memory

#Load Libraries
rm(list = ls()) # clean memory
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)

I will now load my data

#Load Data


file_path <- "C:/Users/lgianetti/OneDrive - City of Cambridge/DACSS Program/690V_data_viz/hw-4/Mass_Arrests.xlsx"

my_data <- read_excel(path = file_path,
    sheet = 1, col_names = TRUE, col_types = NULL, na = "", skip = 0)

I will do a quick look at the data

#Look at Data
str(my_data, width = 70,strict.width='cut')
tibble [8,622 × 10] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ Agency                    : chr [1:8622] "MSP - DFS" "MSP - DFS""..
 $ Incident Number           : chr [1:8622] "2019-0D4-000522" "2019"..
 $ Arrest Date               : POSIXct[1:8622], format: "2019-01-25"..
 $ Arrest Location           : chr [1:8622] "MIDDLEBOROUGH" "WESTBO"..
 $ Arrest Offense by UCR Code: chr [1:8622] "MV" "90Z" "MV" "MV" ...
 $ Arrest Type               : chr [1:8622] NA NA NA NA ...
 $ Age                       : num [1:8622] 31 31 59 48 32 22 51 58 ..
 $ Sex                       : chr [1:8622] "F" "F" "F" "F" ...
 $ Race                      : chr [1:8622] "H" "H" "W" "W" ...
 $ Ethnicity                 : chr [1:8622] "BRA" "BRA" "IRE" "BRA" ..

Now I will clean the data by correcting the data types and checking for missing values and outliers. I will also convert the appropriate columns to factors and numeric types as needed.

#Clean Data
my_data$`Arrest Location` <- factor(my_data$`Arrest Location`)
my_data$`Arrest Offense by UCR Code` <- factor(my_data$`Arrest Offense by UCR Code`)
my_data$`Arrest Type` <- factor(my_data$`Arrest Type`)
my_data$`Sex` <- factor(my_data$`Sex`)
my_data$`Race` <- factor(my_data$`Race`)
my_data$`Ethnicity` <- factor(my_data$`Ethnicity`)
my_data$`Agency` <- factor(my_data$`Agency`)
my_data$`Incident Number` <- factor(my_data$`Incident Number`)



str(my_data, width = 70,strict.width='cut')
tibble [8,622 × 10] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
 $ Agency                    : Factor w/ 1 level "MSP - DFS": 1 1 1 ..
 $ Incident Number           : Factor w/ 2940 levels "2019-0A1-0000"..
 $ Arrest Date               : POSIXct[1:8622], format: "2019-01-25"..
 $ Arrest Location           : Factor w/ 219 levels "ABINGTON","ACT"..
 $ Arrest Offense by UCR Code: Factor w/ 44 levels "100","11A","120"..
 $ Arrest Type               : Factor w/ 4 levels "F","M","O","W": N..
 $ Age                       : num [1:8622] 31 31 59 48 32 22 51 58 ..
 $ Sex                       : Factor w/ 3 levels "F","M","U": 1 1 1..
 $ Race                      : Factor w/ 7 levels "B","H","I","J",....
 $ Ethnicity                 : Factor w/ 42 levels "AMI","BD","BRA",..
summary(my_data) # check for missing values and outliers
       Agency            Incident Number  Arrest Date                    
 MSP - DFS:8622   2019-0H7-004498:  36   Min.   :2019-01-01 00:00:00.00  
                  2019-0D4-005296:  29   1st Qu.:2019-04-30 00:00:00.00  
                  2019-0A5-006044:  22   Median :2019-08-11 00:00:00.00  
                  2019-0A2-000824:  21   Mean   :2019-08-12 23:41:27.47  
                  2019-0A6-007453:  21   3rd Qu.:2019-11-27 00:00:00.00  
                  2019-0A1-009207:  19   Max.   :2020-03-28 00:00:00.00  
                  (Other)        :8474                                   
    Arrest Location Arrest Offense by UCR Code Arrest Type      Age        Sex     
 BOSTON     :1245   MV     :4154               F   :1251   Min.   :15.00   F:1030  
 SPRINGFIELD: 535   90Z    :1946               M   :5441   1st Qu.:27.00   M:7366  
 WORCESTER  : 390   90D    : 775               O   : 180   Median :33.00   U: 226  
 LAWRENCE   : 374   35A    : 630               W   :1706   Mean   :34.89           
 REVERE     : 237   520    : 270               NA's:  44   3rd Qu.:41.00           
 STURBRIDGE : 235   (Other): 841                           Max.   :74.00           
 (Other)    :5606   NA's   :   6                           NA's   :5               
 Race       Ethnicity   
 B: 999   PR     :2457  
 H:4862   DR     :1483  
 I:  51   BRA    :1038  
 J: 132   U      : 666  
 O: 387   HT     : 476  
 U:  58   IRE    : 427  
 W:2133   (Other):2075  
# contingency table of counts
# independent variable in the columns - sex
# dependent variable in the rows - Arrest Type
(ArrestSex=table(my_data$'Arrest Type',my_data$Sex))
   
       F    M    U
  F  156 1082   13
  M  605 4698  138
  O   18  156    6
  W  244 1393   69
#choosing to exclude the 44 NA values in the table
#marginal percentage (per column) from contingency table

ArrestSex.prop = prop.table(ArrestSex,2) |> 
  round(3)

ArrestSex.prop
   
        F     M     U
  F 0.152 0.148 0.058
  M 0.591 0.641 0.611
  O 0.018 0.021 0.027
  W 0.239 0.190 0.305
#making a data frame from the contingency table
ArrestSexDF=as.data.frame(ArrestSex)
names(ArrestSexDF)=c("Arrest Type","Sex","counts")

#adding marginal shares as percentages:
ArrestSexDF$pctCol=100*as.data.frame(ArrestSex.prop)[,3]

ArrestSexDF
ggplot(data=ArrestSexDF, 
             aes(x=`Arrest Type`, y=counts)) + geom_bar(stat="identity")


base1=ggplot(data=ArrestSexDF,
             aes(x=`Arrest Type`, y=counts,
                 fill=Sex)) 
barDodge1= base1 +  geom_bar(stat="identity",
                            position ='dodge') 
barDodge1 

#cleaning up the order the arrest type shows in the plot
ArrestSexDF$`Arrest Type` <- factor(
  ArrestSexDF$`Arrest Type`,
  levels = c("M", "W", "F", "O")
)
base1_pct=ggplot(data=ArrestSexDF,
             aes(x=`Arrest Type`, y=pctCol,
                 fill=reorder(Sex,pctCol))) # reordering
barDodge1_pct= base1_pct +  geom_bar(stat="identity",
                            position ='dodge')+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "M" = "Misdemeanor",
    "F" = "Felony",
    "W" = "Warrant",
    "O" = "Other"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "F" = "#0072B2",   # Blue
      "M" = "#E69F00",   # Orange
      "U" = "#009E73"    # Green
    ),
  labels = c(
    "F" = "Female",
    "M" = "Male",
    "U" = "Unknown Gender"
  ))
                            
common_text_size <- 10

barDodge1_pct + labs(title = "Across All Genders, the Distribution of Arrests is Dominated by Misdemeanors",
                 subtitle = "Arrests by type from January 1, 2019 to March 28, 2020",
                 x="",
                 y="Share of all arrests (%)",
                 fill="",
                 caption = "Source: Mass-Arrests.xlsx\n44 cases of unknown arrest type excluded from plot") +  
            theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",      
      size = 16
  )
    ) +
  theme(
    # Axis labels
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    
    # Tick labels
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    
    # Legend text
    legend.text = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    legend.title = element_text(size = common_text_size)
  ) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(20, 40, 60), 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "gray70")


#add 44 cases of unkow type exluded from the plot as a note in the caption

                    
saveRDS(barDodge1_pct, file = "plot1.rds")
#box plot to understand age distribution by arrest type without NA

my_data_no_na <- my_data |>
  filter(!is.na(`Arrest Type`))

overall_median <- median(my_data_no_na$Age, na.rm = TRUE)

order_levels <- my_data_no_na %>%
  group_by(`Arrest Type`) %>%
  summarize(median_age = median(Age, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(median_age) %>%
  pull(`Arrest Type`)

my_data_no_na$`Arrest Type` <- factor(
  my_data_no_na$`Arrest Type`,
  levels = order_levels
)

boxplot_age = ggplot(my_data_no_na, aes(x = `Arrest Type`, y = Age)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "M" = "Misdemeanor",
    "F" = "Felony",
    "W" = "Warrant",
    "O" = "Other"
  )) +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution by Arrest Type",
       x = "Arrest Type",
       y = "Age") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = common_text_size)
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = overall_median,
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "red")
boxplot_age

box_violin <- ggplot(my_data_no_na, aes(x = `Arrest Type`, y = Age)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(20, 40, 60, 80),
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "gray70") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = overall_median,
             linetype = "solid",
             color = "red") +
  geom_violin(fill = "#4FA3D1", alpha = 0.65) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_text(
  stat = "summary",
  fun = median,
  aes(label = round(..y.., 1)),
  vjust = -0.7,
  size = common_text_size / 3) +
  annotate("text",
           x = .585,
           y = overall_median + 1.5,
           label = paste("Median Arrest\nAge =", round(overall_median, 1)),
           color = "red",
           size = 3) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "M" = "Misdemeanor",
    "F" = "Felony",
    "W" = "Warrant",
    "O" = "Other"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Across All Arrest Types, Arrest Age Distributions Cluster in the 20s and 30s",
    subtitle = "Median ages are similar across offense types, with only modest variation",
    x = "",
    y = "Age at Time of Arrest",
    caption = "Source: Mass-Arrests.xlsx\n44 cases of unknown arrest type excluded from plot"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = common_text_size),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = common_text_size)
  )

box_violin

NA
NA
